instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `xor2` that returns true when exactly one of two expressions is truthy. Each expression must be evaluated only once even though both are inspected twice in the resulting boolean logic.
original_failure_log_excerpt:
MISMATCH
EXPECTED: (LET ((#:G1 P) (#:G2 Q))
            (AND (OR #:G1 #:G2) (NOT (AND #:G1 #:G2))))
ACTUAL:   (LET ((#:G112 P) (#:G113 Q))
            (AND (OR #:G112 #:G113) (NOT (AND #:G112 #:G113))))

--- stderr ---
